name: Approval Callback (Telegram callback_query listener for destroy-resource)

parameters.body: ={{ JSON.stringify({ query: 'mutation ServiceDelete($id: String!) { serviceDelete(id: $id) }', variables: { id: $json._resource_id } }) }}

parameters.jsCode:
const ctx = $input.first().json;
return [{ json: { _chat_id: ctx._chat_id, _message_id: ctx._message_id, _cb_id: ctx._cb_id, _toast: 'Destroy denied', text: '🚫 Destroy denied for ' + ctx._resource_id + '.' } }];

const ctx = $input.first().json;
return [{ json: { _chat_id: ctx._chat_id, _message_id: ctx._message_id, _cb_id: ctx._cb_id, _toast: 'Unauthorized', text: '🚫 Unauthorized callback ignored.' } }];

const ctx = $input.first().json;
return [{ json: { _chat_id: ctx._chat_id, _message_id: ctx._message_id, _cb_id: ctx._cb_id, _toast: 'Unknown action', text: '⚠ Unknown callback_data: ' + (ctx._raw ?? '(empty)') } }];

_comment: Passive listener. Pairs with destroy-resource.json (ADR-0005, Option B). Telegram Trigger receives callback_query updates; the Authorize & Parse node enforces the chat.id whitelist (R-09) and parses 'dr:<verb>:<type>:<id>' callback_data; the Switch routes to approve/deny/unauthorized/unknown arms. The approve arm calls Railway GraphQL serviceDelete(id). All four arms converge to Strip Inline Keyboard (editMessageReplyMarkup) → Answer Callback Query → Reply Status, so a second tap on the same message is a no-op even on race. callback_data prefixes consumed: 'dr:a:rs:' (approve) / 'dr:d:rs:' (deny). Required env vars: TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN, TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID, RAILWAY_API_TOKEN. Not a SKILL.md skill — passive trigger, never routed by the Skills Router.
